courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Elementary German
courses.description: Fundamentals of the German language with emphasis on grammar, reading, composition, and conversation. 1105: for students with no prior knowledge of the language; 1106: for students who have completed 1105 or less than three years in high school.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Elementary German
courses.description: Fundamentals of the German language with emphasis on grammar, reading, composition, and conversation. 1105: for students with no prior knowledge of the language; 1106: for students who have completed 1105 or less than three years in high school.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Accelerated Elementary German
courses.description: Proficiency-oriented approach to elementary German, designed for learners who wish to progress rapidly through the beginning stages of language learning. It develops the four language skills (speaking, listening, reading, writing) in a cultural context. Partially duplicates GER 1105 and 1106.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Intermediate German
courses.description: Review of grammar with increasing emphasis on reading, writing, and oral communication.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Intermediate German
courses.description: Review of grammar with increasing emphasis on reading, writing, and oral communication. I,II

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Accelerated Intermediate German
courses.description: Proficiency-oriented approach to intermediate German, designed for learners who wish to progress rapidly through the intermediate stages of language learning. The course strengthens the four language skills (speaking, listening, reading and writing) in a cultural context. Accelerated version of GER 2105-2106.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Introduction to German Culture and Civilization
courses.description: Examination of major German-language cultural movements, works, and figures from the earliest times to the present. Interdisciplinary exploration of German-language literature, film, art, architecture, music, and theatre in the context of the history of the German-speaking world. Analysis of Germanic culture, values and beliefs, and politics in their European and international context. Taught in English.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Grammar, Composition and Conversation
courses.description: Progressive and comprehensive review of German syntax and morphology. Development of written and oral expression. Development of reading and listening skills and introduction to contemporary public debates through the study of original German materials. Understanding of the role of social, historical, political, and cultural contexts and of fact- based reasoning in communication. Introduction to cultural research in German. Taught in German. GER 3105: review of basic and complex grammatical structures; GER 3106: review of advanced grammatical structures, writing intensive.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Grammar, Composition and Conversation
courses.description: Progressive and comprehensive review of German syntax and morphology. Development of written and oral expression. Development of reading and listening skills and introduction to contemporary public debates through the study of original German materials. Understanding of the role of social, historical, political, and cultural contexts and of fact- based reasoning in communication. Introduction to cultural research in German. Taught in German. GER 3105: review of basic and complex grammatical structures; GER 3106: review of advanced grammatical structures, writing intensive.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: German Oral Proficiency
courses.description: For acquisition of measured levels of proficiency in speaking and understanding spoken German. Content-based instruction in small groups. 3125: to achieve an oral proficiency rating comparable to Intermediate-high on the American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages Oral Proficiency Interview (ACTFL-OPI) or S-1+ on the Foreign Service Institute (FSI) scale. 3126: to achieve an oral proficiency rating comparable to Advanced on the (ACTFL-OPI) or a 2 on the FSI scale. Admission by oral exam. Taught alternate years.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: German Oral Proficiency
courses.description: For acquisition of measured levels of proficiency in speaking and understanding spoken German. Content-based instruction in small groups. 3125: to achieve an oral proficiency rating comparable to Intermediate-high on the American Council on the Teaching of Foreign Languages Oral Proficiency Interview (ACTFL-OPI) or S-1+ on the Foreign Service Institute (FSI) scale. 3126: to achieve an oral proficiency rating comparable to Advanced on the (ACTFL-OPI) or a 2 on the FSI scale. Admission by oral exam. Taught alternate years.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Culture of the German-Speaking Countries
courses.description: Study of German, Austrian, and Swiss culture and civilization from the Middle Ages to the present, including literature, art, architecture, film, and music.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: German in a Globalized World
courses.description: Overview of globalization and its impact on society, culture, politics, economy, and environment in the context of German-speaking countries, with a focus on Germany. Examination of the function and circulation of German within the European Union and in global contexts. Analysis of German-language texts, films, and cultural artifacts. Enhancement of German proficiency, intercultural competencies, and research skills. Taught in German.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Topics in German Culture and Literature
courses.description: Introduces students to critical issues in German culture and literature. Familiarizes students with artistic, cultural, and socio-historical contexts necessary to analyze artifacts (canonical and non-canonical literature, film, art, architecture, and music). Develops critical reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German regarding complex texts, contexts, and concepts. Fosters cross-cultural awareness and intercultural sensitivity. 3305: examines pre-20th-century German cultural and literary developments; 3306: examines cultural and literary developments in the 20th and 21st centuries. Taught in German. Variable content. May be repeated once for credit with different content for a maximum of 6 credits.

prerequisites.rawText: (GER 3105 and GER 3106) or (GER 3105 and GER 3204) or (GER 3105 and GER 3306) or (GER 3106 and GER 3204) or (GER 3106 and GER 3306)

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Topics in German Culture and Literature
courses.description: Introduces students to critical issues in German culture and literature. Familiarizes students with artistic, cultural, and socio-historical contexts necessary to analyze artifacts (canonical and non-canonical literature, film, art, architecture, and music). Develops critical reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills in German regarding complex texts, contexts, and concepts. Fosters cross-cultural awareness and intercultural sensitivity. 3305: examines pre-20th-century German cultural and literary developments; 3306: examines cultural and literary developments in the 20th and 21st centuries. Taught in German. Variable content. May be repeated once for credit with different content for a maximum of 6 credits.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Topics in German Cinema
courses.description: Critical issues in the history of German Cinema. Aesthetic characteristics of major periods, with an emphasis on cinematic trends and ways in which films reflect cultural developments in German-speaking countries. Taught in English. Variable content. May be taken twice for credit with different content.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Advanced Composition and Stylistics
courses.description: Intensive advanced work in written German. Development of the students ability to write clear, correct, and articulate German in a variety of modes. Style analysis. Writing intensive.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Studies in 19th-Century Literature
courses.description: Variable content course devoted to the study of 19th century drama, lyric, and prose. May be repeated for credit with different content.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Studies in 20th-Century Literature
courses.description: A variable content course devoted to the study of major literary works of the 20th century. May be repeated for credit with different content.

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Special Topics in German Life, Literature, and Language
courses.description: Variable content course devoted to the study of various aspects of German culture, literature, and language. May be repeated for credit with different content.

prerequisites.rawText: (GER 3105 and GER 3106) and (GER 3204 or GER 3305 or GER 3306)

courses.subject: GER
courses.title: Senior Tutorial in German Studies
courses.description: Individual or small group sessions which give the student the opportunity to hone special language skills, with a focus on post-graduation application of these skills. May concentrate on areas such as technical or business language, linguistics, translation, interpreting, creative writing, specialized literary, or cultural studies. Must be pre-arranged three weeks before end of previous semester. May be taken twice for credit with different content. PRE: one 4000-level German course, major with senior standing, and consent required.
